flavorText: Berserkers abandon caution in pursuit of relentless rage and brutal retaliation.

features.description: While raging, you can make one extra unarmed strike as a bonus action on each of your turns. When your rage ends, you gain one level of Exhaustion.
While raging, you are immune to the charmed and frightened conditions.
As an action, choose a creature within 30 feet that can see or hear you. It must succeed on a Wisdom save (DC 8 + proficiency bonus + Charisma modifier) or be frightened of you.
When a creature within your reach damages you, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature.

berserker-frenzy.name: Frenzy
berserker-frenzy.summary: Your rage can convert spare momentum into an extra burst of close-range violence.
berserker-frenzy.description:
While raging, you can make one extra unarmed strike as a bonus action on each of your turns. When your rage ends, you gain one level of Exhaustion.

Frenzy turns an already-aggressive barbarian turn into even more pressure by chaining additional offense into your rage state. The important in-app job is making it clear that this is a real turn-shaping button, not just a subclass label.

The sheet should make the specialization identity obvious, not bury it as a hidden unlock.

berserker-mindless-rage.name: Mindless Rage
berserker-mindless-rage.summary: While raging, mental interference is far less likely to take you out of the fight.
berserker-mindless-rage.description:
While raging, you are immune to the charmed and frightened conditions.

Mindless Rage protects the berserker from fear, charm, and similar control at the exact moment front-line commitment matters most.

The sheet should make the specialization identity obvious, not bury it as a hidden unlock.

berserker-intimidating-presence.name: Intimidating Presence
berserker-intimidating-presence.summary: Project primal menace so hard that enemies can buckle under a fear effect.
berserker-intimidating-presence.description:
As an action, choose a creature within 30 feet that can see or hear you. It must succeed on a Wisdom save (DC 8 + proficiency bonus + Charisma modifier) or be frightened of you.

Intimidating Presence is a control action, not flavor text. Use it when shutting down a key enemy is worth more than one more normal attack.

The sheet should make the specialization identity obvious, not bury it as a hidden unlock.

berserker-retaliation.name: Retaliation
berserker-retaliation.summary: When foes hurt you in melee, you can answer with immediate violence instead of waiting for your next turn.
berserker-retaliation.description:
When a creature within your reach damages you, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature.

Retaliation makes the berserker punishing to engage and rewards enemies for choosing the wrong target. The sheet should surface this as a real reaction option during combat.

The sheet should make the specialization identity obvious, not bury it as a hidden unlock.
berserker-retaliation.trigger: A creature within reach damages you.
